title: Top Quantum Computing Companies - by Russ Fein
why:
- There is not yet clear, independently verified evidence that a quantum computer can do something commercially useful that a classical computer cannot already do.
- A recent landscape counts around 80+ quantum processing unit companies worldwide, with nearly 200 quantum-focused firms tracked more broadly.
- DARPA’s Quantum Benchmarking Initiative (QBI) is explicitly investigating whether anyone can build a “utility-scale” quantum computer whose computational value exceeds its cost on useful problems by 2033.
- Key companies leading the race to commercial utility include IonQ, IBM Quantum, Google Quantum AI, and Quantinuum, each with specific milestones in hardware, error correction, and commercial progress.
- Investments and acquisitions are accelerating, with IonQ becoming the first pure-play quantum company to surpass $130 million in GAAP revenue and raising nearly $3 billion in capital.
